name: Divided section
description: The divided section pattern is used to separate related content into distinct steps or items within a single section. Each item is visually divided using a muted [Rule](/sites/construct/divided-section#anatomy) and shallow padding, helping users distinguish between content blocks while maintaining a cohesive layout.
usage:
The divided section pattern provides a flexible layout for presenting grouped content that needs clear internal separation, such as steps, stages, or sequential elements. Each [Divided block](/sites/construct/divided-section#anatomy) is separated by a muted [Rule](/sites/construct/divided-section#anatomy) and shallow padding, creating visual structure without introducing strong section breaks.

The pattern can accommodate a wide range of content types, including text, images, videos, lists, code blocks, logos, and call-to-action elements. Both the section description area and the [Divided block](/sites/construct/divided-section#anatomy)s themselves support these content types.

Internally, the divided section reuses content blocks from the [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) pattern. As a result, most layout configurations and content options behave identically to those in [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section), ensuring consistency and reducing the need for additional configuration or learning.

### Layout

Divided section adapts across screen sizes. On medium screens, the layout is configurable: content can split(50/50) or stack vertically(full-width). On small screens, content always stacks.

### **Supported UI blocks for the** **[Content area](/sites/construct/divided-section#anatomy)**

1.  **Description / body text** - Use for long-form descriptions or explanations, when full sentences and paragraphs work best.
2.  **Image** - Include a 3:2 or 16:9 ratio image to illustrate or enhance the content including: photography, isometric illustrations, diagrams, and similar visuals. The image can include an optional caption.
3.  **Video** - Embed and link video content in 16:9 ratio.
4.  **[List](/global/construct/list)** - List up to 10 items, with bullet type options including: none, bullet, status, or number.
5.  **Code block** - Display preformatted code.
6.  **[Logo block](/sites/construct/logo-block)** - Display related logos such as customers or partners.
7.  **[Linked logo block](/sites/construct/linked-logo-block)** - Display logos that link to additional resources.
8.  **[CTA block](/sites/construct/cta-block)** - Share one or more call-to-action elements, including a primary button, secondary button, and link.
9.  **[Notification](/global/construct/notification)** - Highlight alerts, updates, or contextual messages within the section.

### **When to use**

*   When content needs to be broken into clear steps or distinct items within a single section
*   When a subtle visual separation is needed without creating fully independent sections

### **When not to use**

*   When images, lists, or other content need to stand alone without internal dividers, use [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) instead
*   When the content consists of text only, use a [Tiered list](/sites/construct/tiered-list) instead
*   When the content is made up of short text and a single representative image, consider using [Equal heights](/sites/construct/equal-heights) instead

table.name: Rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new section.

table.name: Heading
table.description: The section heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Description block
table.description: The description provides introductory context for the divided section. It appears at the top of the section and helps users understand the purpose or scope of the content before engaging with the divided blocks.

table.name: Divided block
table.description: The divided block represents a single step or content item within the section. Each block is visually separated by a muted rule and shallow padding, creating clear distinction between items while maintaining a cohesive flow.

table.name: Block rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new block item.

table.name: Block heading
table.description: The block heading identifies the focus of each [Divided block](wiki-ref://grid-YfHOdsWGM_/i-DUwUcIMqO2).

table.name: Content area
table.description: The content area contains the main content for each [Divided block](wiki-ref://grid-YfHOdsWGM_/i-DUwUcIMqO2). It supports the same content types and layout options as the [Basic section](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-vpN6ETB7hS) pattern, including text, images, videos, lists, code blocks, logos, and CTA block.

properties.name: Section rule
properties.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new pattern
properties.constraint: Only use the default style for the section-level rule

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Medium layout
properties.description: Indicate layout for the medium dimension

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

properties.name: Block rule
properties.description: The rule component to separate each  block

properties.name: Heading
properties.description: 4-column width H5 heading for divided block

properties.name: Bullet style
properties.description: The bullet style defines how the block heading is visually presented within each divided block. When the bullet option is used, an additional 32px indent is applied to the content area below the heading to align the content visually with the bullet marker.

properties.name: Block item bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the block item
properties.constraint: The last block item of the pattern/block must not have bottom padding.
